hypothesis: If LoQI's stereochemistry-aware diffusion model (with auxiliary edges for E/Z and directed cycles for R/S) is used to generate conformers from noised inputs, then the resulting conformers will have energies closer to near-DFT minima and higher stereochemical accuracy (R/S and E/Z) with preserved topology than conformers generated without such augmentation, because the augmentation enforces correct stereochemical relationships and guides diffusion toward energetically favorable geometries.
